chosen_reason: Prophet and SARIMA tied on accuracy in the analysis (MAE $14,501 vs $14,601, both ~17.8% MAPE on a held-out 6-month test) -- a real production tradeoff followed from there: Prophet needs a compiled Stan backend and SARIMA needs a heavier statistics library, both unnecessary weight for a tied-accuracy choice. The deployed API instead uses a direct trend + seasonal-index implementation (linear trend fit plus average per-month seasonal deviation) -- the same core idea both models rely on, implemented with zero extra dependencies, and it verified at MAE $13,626 / 16.7% MAPE on the identical held-out test, competitive with both.
